---
title: "Stats Modeling and Computing Final Project"
author: "Avi Kodali(ank93) and Vatsal Malkari(vm593)"
date: "2026-04-01"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(boot)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
```

```{r}
df <- read.csv("marketing_campaign.csv", sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

```{r}
df_clean <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Age = 2014 - Year_Birth,
    Income = ifelse(is.na(Income), median(Income, na.rm = TRUE), Income)
  ) %>%
  filter(Age >= 18 & Age <= 100) %>%
  select(-Z_CostContact, -Z_Revenue) 
df_clean %>% head()
```


```{r}
spending_cols <- c("MntWines", "MntFruits", "MntMeatProducts", "MntFishProducts", "MntSweetProducts", "MntGoldProds")
pca_result <- prcomp(df_clean[, spending_cols], center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
summary(pca_result)
```

```{r}
df_clean$Spend_PC1 <- pca_result$x[, 1]
df_clean$Spend_PC2 <- pca_result$x[, 2]
```

```{r}
numeric_features <- c("Age", "Spend_PC1", "Spend_PC2",
                      "Recency", "NumWebPurchases", "NumCatalogPurchases",
                      "NumStorePurchases", "NumWebVisitsMonth")

non_pc <- setdiff(numeric_features, c("Spend_PC1", "Spend_PC2", "Spend_PC3"))
df_scaled <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(non_pc), ~ as.numeric(scale(.)))) %>% 
  select(-Income)
```


```{r}
set.seed(123)
wss <- sapply(1:12, function(k) {
  kmeans(df_scaled[, numeric_features],
         centers = k, nstart = 25, iter.max = 50)$tot.withinss
})

plot(1:12, wss, type="b", xlab="Number of Clusters (k)", ylab="Within cluster Sum of Squares", main=" K-means", pch=19, col="blue")
text(1:12, wss, labels=round(wss,1), pos=3, cex=0.8)
wss_diff <- diff(wss)
wss_diff
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(df_scaled[, numeric_features], centers = 4, nstart = 25, iter.max = 50)

df_clean$Cluster <- kmeans_result$cluster

df_clean %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarize(
    Avg_Age = mean(Age),
    Avg_Income = mean(Income),
    Avg_Spend_PC1 = mean(Spend_PC1),
    Avg_Spend_PC2 = mean(Spend_PC2),
    Avg_Recency = mean(Recency),
    Avg_WebPurchases = mean(NumWebPurchases),
    Avg_CatalogPurchases = mean(NumCatalogPurchases),
    Avg_StorePurchases = mean(NumStorePurchases),
    Avg_WebVisits = mean(NumWebVisitsMonth),
    Avg_Response = mean(Response)
  )
```

```{r}
df_clean %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarize(Avg_Income = mean(Income)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(Cluster), y = Avg_Income, fill = factor(Cluster))) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Average Income by Cluster", x = "Cluster", y = "Income") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Spend_PC1, y = Spend_PC2, color = factor(Cluster))) +
geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + labs(title = "Customer Clusters", x = "Spend_PC1", y = "Spend_PC2", color = "Cluster")
```

```{r}
cluster_summary <- df_clean %>% group_by(Cluster) %>%
summarize( Responded = sum(Response), Total = n())

cluster_summary
```

```{r}
high_value_clusters <- c(1, 2, 4)

resp_rate <- function(data, indices) {
  mean(data[indices])
}

bootstrap_results <- list()

for(i in 1:4) {
  cluster_data <- df_clean %>% filter(Cluster == i) %>% pull(Response)
  boot_obj <- boot(cluster_data, resp_rate, R = 5000)
  boot_ci <- boot.ci(boot_obj, type = "perc")
  
  bootstrap_results[[i]] <- data.frame(Cluster = i, Response_Rate = boot_obj$t0, Boot_SE = sd(boot_obj$t), CI_Lower = boot_ci$percent[4], CI_Upper = boot_ci$percent[5])
}

bootstrap_summary <- do.call(rbind, bootstrap_results)
print(bootstrap_summary)
```


```{r}
cluster_summary <- df_clean %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% summarize( Responded = sum(Response), Total = n(), Response_Rate = Responded / Total )
cluster_summary
```



```{r}
response_rate <- function(data, indices) mean(data[indices])

set.seed(123)
per_cluster_bootstrap <- list()

for (i in 1:4) {
  cluster_responses <- df_clean %>% filter(Cluster == i) %>% pull(Response)
  boot_obj <- boot(cluster_responses, response_rate, R = 5000)
  ci_perc <- boot.ci(boot_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]

  per_cluster_bootstrap[[i]] <- data.frame(
    Cluster = i,
    N_Customers = length(cluster_responses),
    Response_Rate = boot_obj$t0,
    Boot_SE = sd(boot_obj$t),
    CI_Lower = ci_perc[1],
    CI_Upper = ci_perc[2]
  )
}

cluster_response_summary <- do.call(rbind, per_cluster_bootstrap)
print(cluster_response_summary)

ggplot(cluster_response_summary, aes(x = factor(Cluster), y = Response_Rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue4", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 4, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean(df_clean$Response), linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Per-Cluster Response Rates with 95% Bootstrap CIs",
    subtitle = "Red dashed = overall response rate",
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Response Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

```{r}
high_value_responses <- df_clean$Response[df_clean$Cluster %in% high_value_clusters]
other_cluster_responses <- df_clean$Response[!(df_clean$Cluster %in% high_value_clusters)]

n_high_value  <- length(high_value_responses)
n_other <- length(other_cluster_responses)

observed_rate_diff <- mean(high_value_responses) - mean(other_cluster_responses)

set.seed(123)
B <- 5000
bootstrap_rate_diffs <- numeric(B)

for (i in 1:B) {
  high_value_resample  <- sample(high_value_responses,    n_high_value, replace = TRUE)
  other_resample <- sample(other_cluster_responses, n_other,      replace = TRUE)
  bootstrap_rate_diffs[i] <- mean(high_value_resample) - mean(other_resample)
}

alpha <- 0.05
bootstrap_se <- sd(bootstrap_rate_diffs)


ci_percentile <- quantile(bootstrap_rate_diffs, c(alpha/2, 1 - alpha/2))

cat("\n=== TWO-SAMPLE BOOTSTRAP: HIGH-VALUE vs OTHER CLUSTERS ===\n")
cat("Mean response rate (high-value clusters):", round(mean(high_value_responses), 4), "\n")
cat("Mean response rate (other clusters): ", round(mean(other_cluster_responses), 4), "\n")
cat("Observed difference in rates: ", round(observed_rate_diff, 4), "\n")
cat("Bootstrap SE: ", round(bootstrap_se, 4), "\n\n")

cat("Percentile 95% CI: [", round(ci_percentile[1], 4), ",", round(ci_percentile[2], 4), "]\n")

ggplot(data.frame(rate_diff = bootstrap_rate_diffs), aes(x = rate_diff)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", colour = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = observed_rate_diff, colour = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = ci_percentile, colour = "darkgreen",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "black", linetype = "dotted") +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrap Distribution: Difference in Response Rates",
    subtitle = paste0("Red = observed (", round(observed_rate_diff, 3), ") | Green dashed = 95% percentile CI | Dotted = null"),
    x = "Difference in Response Rate (high-value, other)",
    y = "Count"
    ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```


```{r}
n_contacted_targeted <- sum(df_clean$Cluster %in% high_value_clusters)
n_contacted_mass <- nrow(df_clean)

set.seed(123)
B <- 5000
targeted_strategy_rate <- numeric(B)
mass_strategy_rate <- numeric(B)

for (i in 1:B) {
  high_value_resample <- sample(high_value_responses, n_high_value, replace = TRUE)
  full_dataset_resample <- sample(df_clean$Response, n_contacted_mass, replace = TRUE)
  targeted_strategy_rate[i] <- mean(high_value_resample)
  mass_strategy_rate[i] <- mean(full_dataset_resample)
}

cat("Mass strategy (target all): ", round(mean(mass_strategy_rate), 4), " 95% CI: [", round(quantile(mass_strategy_rate, 0.025), 4), ",", round(quantile(mass_strategy_rate, 0.975), 4), "]\n")
cat("Targeted (high-value clusters):", round(mean(targeted_strategy_rate), 4), " 95% CI: [", round(quantile(targeted_strategy_rate, 0.025), 4), ",", round(quantile(targeted_strategy_rate, 0.975), 4), "]\n\n")

expected_responses_mass <- mean(mass_strategy_rate) * n_contacted_mass
expected_responses_targeted <- mean(targeted_strategy_rate) * n_contacted_targeted

cat("Customers contacted — mass strategy: ", n_contacted_mass, "\n")
cat("Customers contacted — targeted strategy:", n_contacted_targeted, " (",round(100 * n_contacted_targeted / n_contacted_mass, 1), "% of total)\n")
cat("Expected responses  — mass strategy: ", round(expected_responses_mass), "\n")
cat("Expected responses  — targeted strategy:", round(expected_responses_targeted), " (",round(100 * expected_responses_targeted / expected_responses_mass, 1), "% of mass-targeting)\n")
```

```{r}
quantile(df_clean$Income, probs = c(0.33, 0.67))
```


```{r}
df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Targeted = as.integer(Cluster %in% high_value_clusters),
    Income_Group = case_when(
      Income < 40000 ~ "Low",
      Income <= 63000 ~ "Medium",
      TRUE ~ "High"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
income_vs_targeted <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(Income_Group) %>%
  summarise(Targeting_Rate = round(mean(Targeted), 3), N = n())

income_vs_response <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(Income_Group) %>%
  summarise(Response_Rate = round(mean(Response), 3), N = n())

cat("Income → Targeting rate:\n")
print(income_vs_targeted)
cat("\nIncome → Response rate:\n")
print(income_vs_response)

raw_means <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(Targeted) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Response = mean(Response), N = n())

raw_diff <- diff(raw_means$Mean_Response)
cat("\nRaw difference in means (biased):", round(raw_diff, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
strata <- c("Low", "Medium", "High")

stratum_results <- lapply(strata, function(g) {
  d  <- df_clean %>% filter(Income_Group == g)
  y1 <- d$Response[d$Targeted == 1]
  y0 <- d$Response[d$Targeted == 0]
  n1 <- length(y1)
  n0 <- length(y0)

  psi_hat <- mean(y1) - mean(y0)
  se_g <- sqrt(var(y1)/n1 + var(y0)/n0) 
  ci_lo <- psi_hat - 1.96 * se_g
  ci_hi <- psi_hat + 1.96 * se_g

  cat("\nIncome group:", g, "\n")
  cat("  n_treated =", n1, " | n_control =", n0, "\n")
  cat("  Mean(Y | A=1) =", round(mean(y1), 4)," | Mean(Y | A=0) =", round(mean(y0), 4), "\n")
  cat("  ATE estimate:", round(psi_hat, 4), " | 95% CI: [", round(ci_lo, 4), ",", round(ci_hi, 4), "]\n")

  data.frame(
    Income_Group = g,
    n_treated = n1, n_control = n0,
    mean_y1 = mean(y1), mean_y0 = mean(y0),
    ATE = psi_hat,
    SE  = se_g,
    CI_lower = ci_lo, CI_upper = ci_hi
  )
})

stratum_df <- bind_rows(stratum_results) %>%
  mutate(
    N_stratum = n_treated + n_control,
    Weight = N_stratum / sum(N_stratum)
  )

stratum_df_overlap <- stratum_df %>% filter(Income_Group != "High")

weights_restricted <- stratum_df_overlap$N_stratum / sum(stratum_df_overlap$N_stratum)

overall_ATE_restricted <- sum(stratum_df_overlap$ATE * weights_restricted)
overall_SE_restricted  <- sqrt(sum(weights_restricted^2 * stratum_df_overlap$SE^2))
overall_CI_lo_r <- overall_ATE_restricted - 1.96 * overall_SE_restricted
overall_CI_hi_r <- overall_ATE_restricted + 1.96 * overall_SE_restricted

cat("Weighted ATE:    ", round(overall_ATE_restricted, 4), "\n")
cat("95% CI: [", round(overall_CI_lo_r, 4), ",", round(overall_CI_hi_r, 4), "]\n")
cat("Estimand: ATE among customers with Income < $70K\n")
cat("(", sum(stratum_df_overlap$N_stratum), "of", sum(stratum_df$N_stratum), "customers)\n")
```

```{r}
df_overlap <- df_clean %>% filter(Income_Group != "High")
strata_overlap <- c("Low", "Medium")

compute_weighted_ate_overlap <- function(data) {
  s <- lapply(strata_overlap, function(g) {
    d <- data %>% filter(Income_Group == g)
    y1 <- d$Response[d$Targeted == 1]
    y0 <- d$Response[d$Targeted == 0]
    if (length(y1) == 0 || length(y0) == 0) {
      return(data.frame(ATE = 0, N = nrow(d)))
    }
    data.frame(ATE = mean(y1) - mean(y0), N = nrow(d))
  })
  s <- bind_rows(s)
  w <- s$N / sum(s$N)
  sum(s$ATE * w)
}

t_obs_overlap <- abs(compute_weighted_ate_overlap(df_overlap))

set.seed(123)
B <- 5000
t_perm_overlap <- numeric(B)

for (b in 1:B) {
  df_perm <- df_overlap %>%
    group_by(Income_Group) %>%
    mutate(Targeted = sample(Targeted)) %>%
    ungroup()
  t_perm_overlap[b] <- abs(compute_weighted_ate_overlap(df_perm))
}

p_value_overlap <- mean(t_perm_overlap >= t_obs_overlap)
cat("Restricted permutation p-value (Low + Medium only):", round(p_value_overlap, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
stratum_df %>% 
  filter(Income_Group != "High") %>%  
  ggplot(aes(x = Income_Group, y = ATE)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_lower, ymax = CI_upper), width = 0.15, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = overall_ATE_restricted, linetype = "dotted", colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Stratum-Level ATE Estimates with 95% CIs",
    subtitle = paste0("Red dashed = null (ATE=0) | Green dotted = overall weighted ATE (", round(overall_ATE_restricted, 3), ")"),
    x = "Income Group",
    y = "Estimated ATE (probability scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

hist(t_perm_overlap,
     breaks = 60,
     xlim = c(0, max(t_obs_overlap, max(t_perm_overlap)) * 1.05),
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "white",
     main = "Permutation Distribution Under Sharp Null",
     xlab = "|Weighted ATE|",
     sub = paste0("Red dashed = observed (", round(t_obs_overlap, 4), ")  |  p-value = ", round(p_value_overlap, 4)))
abline(v = t_obs_overlap, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```

```{r}
cat("Bootstrap evidence:\n")
cat("  - High-value clusters (1, 2, 4) response rate:", round(mean(high_value_responses) * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  - Other clusters (3) response rate: ", round(mean(other_cluster_responses) * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  - Difference in response rates: ", round(observed_rate_diff, 4), "\n")
cat("  - Percentile 95% CI for difference: [", round(ci_percentile[1], 4), ",", round(ci_percentile[2], 4), "]\n")
cat("\nCausal association evidence:\n")
cat("  - Stratified ATE (controlling for income):", round(overall_ATE_restricted, 4), "\n")
cat("  - 95% CI for stratified ATE: [", round(overall_CI_lo_r, 4), ",", round(overall_CI_hi_r, 4), "]\n")
cat("  - Permutation p-value: ", round(p_value_overlap, 4), "\n")
```


```{r}
df_clean <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(Edu_Num = as.integer(factor(Education, levels = c("Basic", "2n Cycle", "Graduation", "Master", "PhD"))),
    Married = as.integer(Marital_Status %in% c("Married", "Together")),
    Children = Kidhome + Teenhome
  )

covars <- c("Income", "Age", "Edu_Num", "Married", "Children", "Recency", "NumWebPurchases", "NumCatalogPurchases", "NumStorePurchases", "NumWebVisitsMonth")

df_model <- df_clean %>%
  select(Response, Targeted, all_of(covars)) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(covars), ~ . - mean(.), .names = "c_{.col}"))

centered <- paste0("c_", covars)

formula_lin <- as.formula(
  paste("Response ~ Targeted * (", paste(centered, collapse = " + "), ")")
)

lin_fit <- lm(formula_lin, data = df_model)

robust <- coeftest(lin_fit, vcov = vcovHC(lin_fit, type = "HC2"))

ate <- coef(lin_fit)["Targeted"]
se <- robust["Targeted", "Std. Error"]
ci_low <- ate - 1.96 * se
ci_high <- ate + 1.96 * se

cat("\n  regression-adjusted ATE (Lin estimator)  \n")
cat("ATE: ", round(ate, 4), "\n")
cat("SE: ", round(se, 4), "\n")
cat("95% CI: [", round(ci_low, 4), ",", round(ci_high, 4), "]\n")
cat("p-value:", round(robust["Targeted", "Pr(>|t|)"], 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
ate_comparison <- data.frame(Method = c("Raw Difference", "Restricted Stratified ATE", "Lin Estimator"),
  ATE = c(observed_rate_diff, overall_ATE_restricted,ate),
  CI_Lower = c(ci_percentile[1], overall_CI_lo_r, ci_low),
  CI_Upper = c(ci_percentile[2], overall_CI_hi_r, ci_high)
)
```

```{r}
ggplot(ate_comparison %>% filter(!is.na(CI_Lower)),
       aes(x = Method, y = ATE)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
  coord_flip() + labs(title = "ATE Estimates Across Adjustment Methods",
    subtitle = "Effect shrinks after adjusting for recency & activity",
    x = NULL, y = "Estimated ATE") + theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```
